behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (dispatcher -> three reviewer clients), not model judgment. Deterministic plumbing quote: "sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack." Model-output exclusion quote: this behavior checks what is sent, not whether reviewers are correct. Unit was considered, but rejected because the realistic miss is wiring across multiple reviewer adapters in parallel.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process fakes/spies for extractor/risk/compliance clients; assert each received identical document bytes/id and the correct role-specific instruction pack, with no cross-role mixups during parallel dispatch.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: "live clause extractor identifies key terms" depends on real model extraction quality; stubbed outputs would hide failures in recognition/recall. Not Workflow because this is one reviewer decision, not multiple agentic stages chained.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real clause-extractor model on representative contracts with labeled key terms; assert required entities (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) are extracted with acceptable recall/precision thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic invocation plumbing at a module boundary. Deterministic plumbing quote: "configuration is passed correctly ... invocation." Model-output exclusion quote: behavior does not ask whether risk judgments are correct, only that policy payload is forwarded correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator to a fake risk-assessor client and execute dispatch; verify serialized request includes the exact configured policy fields/versions and no dropped or defaulted policy values.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: "live risk assessor flags ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing" is model semantic judgment; mocks would always pass and miss true failures on nuanced wording. Not Workflow: single reviewer output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk-assessor model on a curated corpus containing direct and euphemistic risky language; score detection quality against adjudicated labels and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in local module wiring/payload mapping. Deterministic plumbing quote: "are passed correctly ... invocation." Model-output exclusion quote: this behavior targets parameter propagation, not compliance decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestrator with a fake compliance-checker endpoint/client and assert request contains the exact jurisdiction code and regulatory profile fields selected for the run.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: "live compliance checker correctly determines" depends on real AI decision correctness against regulations; deterministic stubs cannot validate that judgment quality. Not Workflow because this behavior isolates one reviewer’s decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes for configured requirements; assert decision accuracy and critical-requirement recall meet acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Core failure target is deterministic merge logic in one local boundary. Deterministic plumbing quote: "merge step preserves both assessments with attribution." Model-output exclusion quote: inputs already contain conflicting assessments; test does not depend on how models produced them. Higher levels were considered but rejected because behavior text scopes to merge-step conflict handling, not upstream transport/runtime.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with fixed reviewer outputs for the same clause carrying conflicting labels; assert merged report includes both labels and correct source attribution without overwrite.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-stage and includes multiple live AI reviewer steps in sequence/parallel plus merge. "dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report" requires validating handoff across multiple agentic decisions; lower levels can miss cross-stage loss of findings.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts using live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers; verify all reviewer findings are present in final merged report, including per-reviewer attribution and no dropped items across parallel fan-out/fan-in.
